name: PERCENTILE_CONT
shortDescription: Continuous (interpolated) percentile of a numeric column
syntax: PERCENTILE_CONT(p) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY column) OVER (PARTITION BY partition_expr, ...)
PERCENTILE_CONT(p) OVER (PARTITION BY partition_expr, ... ORDER BY column)
description: Computes a continuous (interpolated) percentile. Maps to the Elasticsearch `percentiles` aggregation (TDigest). p is a literal between 0 and 1, e.g. 0.99 for p99. The value column is given by the ORDER BY clause (WITHIN GROUP or OVER); grouping is given by OVER (PARTITION BY ...) or a top-level GROUP BY. A column-first shorthand PERCENTILE_CONT(column, p) is also accepted (many BI tools emit it).

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Value column (from the ORDER BY clause, or the shorthand first argument)

examples.title: p99 latency per endpoint
examples.sql: SELECT endpoint, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.99) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY duration_ms) AS p99 FROM requests GROUP BY endpoint

examples.title: OVER form
examples.description: Partition and value column in OVER
examples.sql: SELECT name, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.99) OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary) AS p99 FROM emp

examples.title: Shorthand
examples.sql: SELECT department, PERCENTILE_CONT(salary, 0.95) AS p95 FROM emp GROUP BY department

notes: Maps to the Elasticsearch `percentiles` aggregation (TDigest, continuous/interpolated).
The value column comes from the ORDER BY clause (WITHIN GROUP or OVER), or the (column, p) shorthand's first argument.
Multiple percentile calls on the same column coalesce into a single Elasticsearch aggregation.
